title: Operand-type-aware error messages — runtime type-check sites
goal: Every runtime type-check failure currently emits `Type error in <op>: expected <expected>`. Extend each site to also include the actual operand type tag in human-readable form: `Type error in <: expected number, got string`. The operand value is already in scope at every check site; the missing piece is a small helper `eshkol_format_value_type_tag(tagged_value_t)` that returns one of the canonical type names ('integer', 'rational', 'bignum', 'double', 'complex', 'dual-number', 'symbol', 'string', 'character', 'boolean', 'pair', 'vector', 'tensor', 'procedure', 'continuation', 'environment', 'hash-table', 'port', 'handle', 'null', 'logic-var', 'substitution', 'fact', 'knowledge-base', 'factor-graph', 'workspace').
next_actions: Add `eshkol_format_value_type_tag(tagged_value_t v) -> const char*` to lib/core/runtime_errors_hosted.cpp.
Audit every `eshkol_raise(..., "Type error in <op>: expected <T>")` call site and rewrite to include the operand type via the new helper.

Add tests/v1_3_edge_cases/operand_type_in_error_test.esk covering: int < string, string + boolean, vector-ref on a number, car on a vector, +/* /min/max with mismatched types, comparison ops, predicates that demand specific types.
Update docs/breakdown/SCHEME_COMPATIBILITY.md's 'Error messages' section to show the new format.
acceptance: Every type-error message in tests/v1_3_edge_cases/operand_type_in_error_test.esk includes the actual operand type as `got <type-name>`.
No type-error message regresses to its previous unhelpful form.
ux_payoff: Highest UX-per-LOC win in v1.3. Every developer who hits a type error today gets a concrete next step instead of guessing which operand was wrong.
